revision.text:
{{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image name = Notojima.jpg | image caption = | image size = | map = Japan | map_caption = Location in Japan | native name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native name link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total islands = | major islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline km = 71.9 | highest mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country admin divisions title = Prefecture | country admin divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country admin divisions title 1 = City | country admin divisions 1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country admin divisions title 2 = | country admin divisions 2 = | country largest city = | country largest city population = | population = 3187 | population as of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Shimane]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting '''Notojima''' to the mainland are trisecting [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] ==History== *1982 - {{nihongo|'''Notojima big bridge'''|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} is complete (southern bridge) *1999 - {{nihongo|'''Twin Bridge Noto'''|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} is complete (northern bridge) *October 1, 2004 - the [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]] has become the part of the [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city and [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. *March 25, 2007 - [[2007 Noto earthquake]] of 6.9 [[Moment magnitude scale]] happened. ==Attractions== '''Notojima''' cuisine is famous for its black burgers.
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] ==See also== *[[Notojima, Ishikawa]] *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notoshima}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image name = Notojima.jpg | image caption = | image size = | map = Japan | map_caption = Location in Japan | native name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native name link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total islands = | major islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline km = 71.9 | highest mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country admin divisions title = Prefecture | country admin divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country admin divisions title 1 = City | country admin divisions 1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country admin divisions title 2 = | country admin divisions 2 = | country largest city = | country largest city population = | population = 3187 | population as of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Shimane]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting '''Notojima''' to the mainland are trisecting [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] ==History== *1982 - {{nihongo|'''Notojima big bridge'''|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} is complete (southern bridge) *1999 - {{nihongo|'''Twin Bridge Noto'''|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} is complete (northern bridge) *October 1, 2004 - the [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]] has become the part of the [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city and [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. *March 25, 2007 - [[2007 Noto earthquake]] of 6.9 [[Moment magnitude scale]] happened. ==Attractions== * At the [[:ja:のとじま臨海公園水族館|Notojima Aquarium]] visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. * The Notojima Glass Museum is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures. *'''Notojima''' cuisine is famous for its black burgers.
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] ==See also== *[[Notojima, Ishikawa]] *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notoshima}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. * The Notojima Glass Art Museum is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *'''Notojima''' cuisine is famous for its black burgers.

{{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image name = Notojima.jpg | image caption = | image size = | map = Japan | map_caption = Location in Japan | native name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native name link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total islands = | major islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline km = 71.9 | highest mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country admin divisions title = Prefecture | country admin divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country admin divisions title 1 = City | country admin divisions 1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country admin divisions title 2 = | country admin divisions 2 = | country largest city = | country largest city population = | population = 3187 | population as of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting '''Notojima''' to the mainland are trisecting [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}。[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos]　The island have both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== *February 1, 1955 - the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]] *1982 - {{nihongo|'''Notojima big bridge'''|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} is complete (southern bridge) *1999 - {{nihongo|'''Twin Bridge Noto'''|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} is complete (northern bridge) *October 1, 2004 - the [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]] was abolished and '''Notojima''' island has become the part of the [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city and [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. *March 25, 2007 - [[2007 Noto earthquake]] of 6.9 [[Moment magnitude scale]] happened. ==Attractions== * At the [[:ja:のとじま臨海公園水族館|Notojima Aquarium]] visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. * The Notojima Glass Art Museum is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *'''Notojima''' cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around '''Notojima''' is a common location for the recreational sailing. ==See also== *[[Notojima, Ishikawa]] *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notoshima}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] (七尾湾) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting '''Notojima''' to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos]　The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== *February 1, 1955 - the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]] *1982 - {{nihongo|'''Notojima big bridge'''|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} is complete (southern bridge) *1999 - {{nihongo|'''Twin Bridge Noto'''|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} is complete (northern bridge) *October 1, 2004 - the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and '''Notojima''' island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. *March 25, 2007 - [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] (のとじま臨海公園水族館) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *'''Notojima''' cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around '''Notojima''' are a common location for recreational sailing. ==See also== *[[Notojima, Ishikawa]] *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==See also== *[[Notojima, Ishikawa]] *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:uncategorized pages]][[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:dd]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:pages from wikipedia]][[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Pages from wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}}

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]]

[[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:tt]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:qq 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]][[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]]

[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Notojima]][[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[en:Notojima]] {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Notojima]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Uncategorized pages]] [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Notojima]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 7]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]]

The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Notojima]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]]

Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Notojima]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]]

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

{{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] [[Category:Pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:pages with valid en interwikis 2]]

Notojima Island is administered as part of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
