[[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[:en:Moment magnitude scale|Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[:en:Notojima Aquarium|Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] }} [[sn:Notojima]] [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]]

[[ceb:Noto Jima]][[sn:commons:Category:Notojima]][[en:Notojima]][[fr:Noto-jima]][[ja:能登島]][[ko:노토섬]][[vi:Notojima]][[zh:能登島]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11612025]] Wikidata name: Notojima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[:en:Moment magnitude scale|Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[:en:Notojima Aquarium|Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] }} [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] {{wikidata link|Q11612025}}

[[ceb:Noto Jima]][[sn:commons:Category:Notojima]][[en:Notojima]][[fr:Noto-jima]][[ja:能登島]][[ko:노토섬]][[vi:Notojima]][[zh:能登島]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11612025]] Wikidata name: Notojima [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]] | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa Prefecture]] | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = [[:en:Japanese people|Japanese]] | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the [[:en:Sea of Japan|Sea of Japan]], less than 500 meters off the coast of the [[:en:Ishikawa Prefecture|Ishikawa]] prefecture in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. Notojima Island is administered as part of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[:en:Moment magnitude scale|Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[:en:Notojima Aquarium|Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] }} [[en:Notojima]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] {{wikidata link|Q11612025}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Notojima Island is administered as part of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[:en:Moment magnitude scale|Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[:en:Notojima Aquarium|Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]

The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima town]]. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of [[:en:Notojima, Ishikawa|Notojima]] was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of [[:en:Nanao, Ishikawa|Nanao]] and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. [[:en:2007 Noto earthquake|An earthquake]] with 6.9 on the [[:en:Moment magnitude scale|Moment magnitude scale]] occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the [[:en:Notojima Aquarium|Notojima Aquarium]] ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch [[:en:Dolphin|Dolphin]] and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[en:Notojima]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] {{wikidata link|Q11612025}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]

ethnic_groups = {{ill|Japanese people|en|Japanese people|WD=Q161652|lt=Japanese}} | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan}}, less than 500 meters off the coast of the {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa}} prefecture in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Notojima Island is administered as part of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao}} city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima town}}. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima}} was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao}} and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. {{ill|2007 Noto earthquake|en|2007 Noto earthquake|WD=Q986103|lt=An earthquake}} with 6.9 on the {{ill|Moment magnitude scale|en|Moment magnitude scale|WD=Q201605|lt=Moment magnitude scale}} occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the {{ill|Notojima Aquarium|en|Notojima Aquarium|WD=Q11275197|lt=Notojima Aquarium}} ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch {{ill|Dolphin|en|Dolphin|WD=Q7369|lt=Dolphin}} and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]

Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = {{ill|Japanese people|en|Japanese people|WD=Q161652|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese people}} | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, less than 500 meters off the coast of the {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} prefecture in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Notojima Island is administered as part of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima town|1=Notojima, Ishikawa}}. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima|1=Notojima, Ishikawa}} was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. {{ill|2007 Noto earthquake|en|2007 Noto earthquake|WD=Q986103|lt=An earthquake|1=2007 Noto earthquake}} with 6.9 on the {{ill|Moment magnitude scale|en|Moment magnitude scale|WD=Q201605|lt=Moment magnitude scale|1=Moment magnitude scale}} occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the {{ill|Notojima Aquarium|en|Notojima Aquarium|WD=Q11275197|lt=Notojima Aquarium|1=Notojima Aquarium}} ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch {{ill|Dolphin|en|Dolphin|WD=Q7369|lt=Dolphin|1=Dolphin}} and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]

[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[en:Notojima]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] {{wikidata link|Q11612025}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Jima]][[en:Notojima]][[fr:Noto-jima]][[ja:能登島]][[ko:노토섬]][[vi:Notojima]][[zh:能登島]] {{Short description|Island in Nanao Bay, Japan}} {{prose|date=April 2022}} {{travel guide|date=April 2022}} {{Infobox islands | name = Notojima Island | image_name = Notojima.jpg | image_caption = | image_size = | map = Japan#Japan Ishikawa Prefecture | map_caption = Location in Japan | native_name = 能登島 ''Notojima'' | native_name_link = Japanese language | nickname = | location = {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}} | coordinates = {{coord|37|07|30|N|136|59|47|E|display=inline,title}} | archipelago = | total_islands = | major_islands = | area_km2 = 46.78 | length_km = 11 | width_km = 2 | coastline_km = 71.9 | highest_mount = mount Yomurazuka | elevation_m = 196.8 | country = Japan | country_admin_divisions_title = Prefecture | country_admin_divisions = {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} | country_admin_divisions_title_1 = City | country_admin_divisions_1 = {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} | country_admin_divisions_title_2 = | country_admin_divisions_2 = | country_largest_city = | country_largest_city_population = | population = 3187 | population_as_of = 2003 | density_km2 = 68.13 | ethnic_groups = {{ill|Japanese people|en|Japanese people|WD=Q161652|lt=Japanese|1=Japanese people}} | additional_info = }} {{nihongo|'''Notojima'''|能登島|Noto-jima}} is a volcanic island in the [[Nanao Bay]] ({{Lang|ja|七尾湾}}) portion of the {{ill|Sea of Japan|en|Sea of Japan|WD=Q27092|lt=Sea of Japan|1=Sea of Japan}}, less than 500 meters off the coast of the {{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|WD=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} prefecture in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Notojima Island is administered as part of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} city. The two bridges connecting Notojima to the mainland trisect [[:ja:七尾湾|Nanao Bay]] into Nanao-North, Nanao-West and Nanao-Nambu bays. The island's highest peak is Mount Yomurazuka at {{convert|196.8|m|ft|abbr=on}}.[http://himalaicus.hiho.jp/yamaseireki/2005/yomuraduka/yomuraduka.html Yomurazuka climbing maps and photos] The island has both elementary and middle schools.[http://www.city.nanao.ishikawa.jp/notojimatyu/ 七尾市立能登島中学校] ==History== On February 1, 1955 the three villages of the island are merged into {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima town|1=Notojima, Ishikawa}}. {{nihongo|Notojima big bridge|能登島大橋|Notojima ohashi}} (southern bridge) was completed in 1982. {{nihongo|Twin Bridge Noto|ツインブリッジ能登|Tsuinburijji Noto}} (northern bridge) was completed in 1999. On October 1, 2004 the town of {{ill|Notojima, Ishikawa|en|Notojima, Ishikawa|WD=Q7063204|lt=Notojima|1=Notojima, Ishikawa}} was abolished and Notojima island became part of the city of {{ill|Nanao, Ishikawa|en|Nanao, Ishikawa|WD=Q695892|lt=Nanao|1=Nanao, Ishikawa}} and of [[:ja:能登半島国定公園|Noto-Hanto Quazi-National Park]]. {{ill|2007 Noto earthquake|en|2007 Noto earthquake|WD=Q986103|lt=An earthquake|1=2007 Noto earthquake}} with 6.9 on the {{ill|Moment magnitude scale|en|Moment magnitude scale|WD=Q201605|lt=Moment magnitude scale|1=Moment magnitude scale}} occurred March 25, 2007. ==Attractions== * At the {{ill|Notojima Aquarium|en|Notojima Aquarium|WD=Q11275197|lt=Notojima Aquarium|1=Notojima Aquarium}} ({{Lang|ja|のとじま臨海公園水族館}}) visitors can watch {{ill|Dolphin|en|Dolphin|WD=Q7369|lt=Dolphin|1=Dolphin}} and [[:en:Seal (mammal)|seal]] shows, animal feeding and meet many species in the 'touch and feel' tank. The aquarium also has a collection of jellyfish. In addition, [[:en:whale sharks|whale sharks]] also live in the facility newly established in 2010. * The [[Notojima Glass Art Museum]] is the only public specialized glass art museum in Japan. The open space outside the Museum also has 14 glass sculptures.[http://nanao-af.jp/glass/ Notojima Glass Art Museum official site] *Notojima cuisine is famous for its black burgers.[http://user.notojima.jp/shimanoyu/ntojimab.html 能登島バーガー]
[[Image:NOTOJIMA Burger (14369881592).jpg|300px]] * The waters around Notojima are a common location for recreational sailing. ==References== *This article incorporates material from Japanese Wikipedia page [[:ja:能登島|能登島]], accessed 10 January 2017 {{Commons Category|Notojima}} {{reflist}} {{Authority control}} {{ishikawa-geo-stub}} [[en:Notojima]] [[ceb:Noto Jima]] {{Commons category|Notojima}} [[fr:Noto-jima]] [[ja:能登島]] [[ko:노토섬]] [[vi:Notojima]] [[zh:能登島]] {{wikidata link|Q11612025}} [[Category:Islands of Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Islands of the Sea of Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
